category_title: Elementary EMLLs exhibiting externalizing behaviors, restlessness, and non-compliance due to language barriers
minimal_context: Shen is standing at the front of the classroom to present a PowerPoint about his vacation to Massachusetts, New York, and Pennsylvania.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Shen, it is time for your presentation. Please show the class your PowerPoint about your vacation to Massachusetts, New York, and Pennsylvania.

STUDENT: (Stands by the board looking shy and nervous. He stays very quiet and looks at the other students in their seats to see what they are doing.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and start. Tell us about the pictures from your trip.

STUDENT: (Looks at the English labels on the screen and shakes his head.) I don’t understand, so I won’t do it.

TUTOR: Just try your best to read the words.

STUDENT: (Becomes antsy, leaves the front of the room, and begins to walk around the classroom without permission.)

options.A: Give the student a warning regarding his behavior of walking around the classroom.
options.B: Offer to stand at the front of the room with the student during the presentation.
options.C: Allow the student to withdraw from the classroom for a time-limited period to prevent emotional overload.
options.D: Provide pictures from his trip and presentation labels in English, Chinese characters, and pinyin.
